fh.shot_strategy: With a 6 o-clock tailwind, LHFH can get extra glide. Use a stable to understable disc and throw with slight anhyzer. This 239ft par 3 hole requires precision.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 8 o-clock, your RHBH fade works with the wind — throw a controlled hyzer with an overstable disc. This 295ft par 3 hole requires precision. Watch for OB areas.

fh.shot_strategy: With a 8 o-clock, RHFH is manageable but requires hyzer angle. Use a stable disc and focus on clean release. This 295ft par 3 hole requires precision. Watch for OB areas.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 8 o-clock, LHBH is challenging with wind pushing your fade further right. Use a very overstable disc. This 295ft par 3 hole requires precision. Watch for OB areas.

fh.shot_strategy: With a 8 o-clock, LHFH is the preferred shot — wind comes across from the left, keeping your flight plate protected. This 295ft par 3 hole requires precision. Watch for OB areas.

wind_info.description: 3 o-clock crosswind from right at 13.7 mph

bh.shot_strategy: With a 3 o-clock crosswind from right, your RHBH flight plate is exposed — prefer RHFH for better control. Use an overstable disc and focus on clean release. This 202ft par 3 hole requires precision.

fh.shot_strategy: With a 3 o-clock crosswind from right, RHFH is the preferred shot. The wind comes across from the right, keeping your flight plate protected. This 202ft par 3 hole requires precision.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 3 o-clock crosswind from right, your LHBH fade works with the wind pushing left — this is a natural combination. This 202ft par 3 hole requires precision.
